title: Fix YouTube OAuth connection state — shows connected then immediately reports disconnected

requirements.summary: Fix the YouTube/Google OAuth connection flow so that after a successful OAuth callback the connection state is correctly persisted and reflected in the UI as connected
acceptanceCriteria: After completing the Google OAuth consent flow and being redirected back to the app, the YouTube channel connection status is shown as connected and remains connected.
The connection status does not flip back to disconnected immediately after a successful OAuth callback.
Refreshing the page after connecting still shows the channel as connected.
The ChannelConnection component correctly reflects the persisted OAuth credentials from the backend.

file_operations.description: Modify the OAuthCallback page to ensure proper state synchronization after successful credential storage. After the storeGoogleOAuthCredentials mutation succeeds, invalidate all YouTube connection-related query cache keys (youtubeConnection, hasGoogleOAuthCredentials, callerUserProfile) to force immediate refetch. Ensure the page waits for the invalidation and refetch to complete before redirecting to the home page, so the UI already shows the connected state when the user lands on the home page.

Review and fix the connection status query logic in useYouTubeChannel to ensure it properly reflects backend state. Verify that the query key is consistent across all call sites and that the enabled condition correctly waits for the actor to be ready. Add explicit query invalidation logic after the disconnect mutation completes successfully to ensure the UI immediately reflects the disconnected state.

Update the ChannelConnection component to handle edge cases where the connection status might be stale immediately after returning from OAuth callback. Add a loading state display during the transition period after OAuth callback and before the connection status query returns fresh data. Ensure the component does not render cached stale connection data from before the OAuth flow started.

requirements.summary: Fix the OAuthCallback page to reliably wait for backend actor initialization before processing and storing OAuth credentials
acceptanceCriteria: The OAuthCallback page does not attempt to store credentials before the actor is fully initialized.
If the actor is not ready within the timeout, a clear error message is shown to the user explaining what happened.
After credentials are stored via the mutation hook, the OAuthCallback page redirects to the main page with connection state already set to connected.
The useYouTubeChannel hook's connection status query is invalidated/refetched after credentials are successfully stored so the UI immediately reflects the connected state.

file_operations.description: Improve the actor readiness check logic to properly detect when the actor is fully initialized and ready to accept credential storage requests. Replace the existing retry logic with a more robust polling mechanism that checks both actor availability and actor.isCallerAdmin or another health-check method to confirm the actor is truly ready. If the actor does not become ready within 15 seconds, display a clear, user-friendly error message explaining that the backend was not ready in time and provide actionable next steps (e.g., retry button or navigate to home and try connecting again).

Review the useStoreGoogleOAuthCredentials mutation hook to ensure it properly handles backend errors and returns clear error messages to the caller. Add error handling for cases where the backend actor is not yet ready or the credential storage fails due to initialization issues. Ensure the mutation's onSuccess callback invalidates all relevant query keys (youtubeConnection, hasGoogleOAuthCredentials, callerUserProfile) so the UI immediately reflects the new connected state.

Review the URL parameter extraction logic to ensure it correctly handles both query string and hash-based routing for OAuth callback parameters (code, state, error, error_description). Add validation to ensure the authorization code and state are present and non-empty before proceeding. Add a helper function to extract error parameters from the OAuth callback URL and return a user-friendly error message if the OAuth provider returned an error.

requirements.summary: Ensure the useYouTubeChannel hook's connection status check query is invalidated and refetched after the OAuth credential save mutation completes successfully
acceptanceCriteria: After the storeGoogleOAuthCredentials mutation succeeds, all YouTube connection-related React Query cache entries are invalidated.
The ChannelConnection component fetches fresh connection status from the backend and displays the channel as connected without requiring a manual page refresh.

file_operations.description: Update the useStoreGoogleOAuthCredentials mutation hook to add an onSuccess callback that uses queryClient.invalidateQueries to invalidate all YouTube connection-related query keys including youtubeConnection, hasGoogleOAuthCredentials, and callerUserProfile. Ensure the invalidation triggers immediate refetch of these queries so any component (like ChannelConnection) that depends on connection status will automatically receive and display the fresh connected state.

Ensure the connection status query (useIsYouTubeChannelConnected) is properly configured with staleTime: 0 or a very short staleTime so it always refetches after invalidation. Verify that the query's enabled condition does not prevent refetch after the credentials are stored. Add a refetchOnMount: 'always' option to ensure the query always fetches fresh data when the ChannelConnection component mounts after returning from the OAuth callback.
